articles.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico star Mora's high school graduation, France-Morocco kicks off quarterfinals
articles.description: Enjoy all the features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition as the first day of the 2026 FIFA World Cup quarterfinals begin.

articles.headline: Morocco press conference chaos leaves Brahim Diaz forgetting the question

images.name: Morocco press conference chaos leaves Brahim Diaz forgetting the question
images.caption: Morocco press conference chaos leaves Brahim Diaz forgetting the question

articles.headline: Lamine Yamal: 'Incredible' to see Lionel Messi still starring at World Cup
articles.description: Spain winger Lamine Yamal has said he is thrilled to see Lionel Messi defy time and continue to have a starring role at this World Cup.

images.name: Lamine Yamal [1296x729]
images.credit: Photo by Grzegorz Wajda/SOPA Images/LightRocket via Getty Images

images.name: Why Spain's substitutes were so important vs. Portugal
images.caption: Alex Kirkland reacts to Spain's late 1-0 win over Portugal to advance to the quarterfinals.

articles.headline: Keir Starmer hints at bank holiday if England win World Cup
articles.description: An England World Cup win could mean an extra bank holiday, Sir Keir Starmer has hinted ahead of a quarterfinal clash with Norway.

images.name: Keir Starmer [1296x729]
images.caption: United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Norway's Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Store greet the media at the Nato summit
images.alt: United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Norway's Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Store greet the media at the Nato summit

images.name: Harry Kane Jude Bellingham [1296x729]
images.caption: Harry Kane (left) and Jude Bellingham (right) celebrate Kane's goal for England on Sunday.

images.name: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'
images.caption: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'

articles.headline: Morocco star Saibari to miss World Cup quarterfinal vs. France
articles.description: Morocco will be without star forward Ismael Saibari for their World Cup quarterfinal with France on Thursday.

images.name: Ismael Saibari [1296x729]
images.caption: Ismael Saibari suffered an injury in Morocco's win over Canada.
images.alt: Ismael Saibari #11 (C) of Morocco reacts after sustaining an injury during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 match between Canada and Morocco at Houston Stadium on July 04, 2026 in Houston, Texas.

article.headline: Kylian Mbappé scores twice in rain-delayed France win over Iraq
article.description: Kylian Mbappé scored twice to move into a tie for second in career World Cup goals with 16, and France played through the tournament's first rain delay to beat Iraq 3-0 on Monday.
article.linkText: Mbappé scores twice in rain-delayed France win

images.name: Iraq coach jokes about France clash: 'I asked to play three goalkeepers'
images.caption: Iraq coach jokes about France clash: 'I asked to play three goalkeepers'

article.story:
Kylian Mbappé scored twice to move into a tie for second in career World Cup goals with 16, and France played through the tournament's first rain delay to beat Iraq 3-0 on Monday and advance to the knockout stage.

Ballon d'Or winner Ousmane Dembélé also scored for Les Bleus, giving fans who stuck out a rainy night in Philadelphia a reason to cut loose.

Mbappé scored in the 14th and 54th minutes to cap a momentous and soggy 100th international appearance for one of the best players in the world.

Mbappe's first goal gave France a 1-0 lead at halftime, when heavy rain and an incoming thunderstorm prompted a delay of just over two hours and forced the grounds crew to squeegee water off the swampy Kentucky bluegrass at Lincoln Financial Field.

Scoreboard messages directed the crowd to take shelter in the stadium's covered areas, warning that a severe storm was on the way. Sheets of rain continued to fall through halftime as fans wrapped themselves in ponchos and huddled in the concourse and under balconies.

Iraq coach Graham Arnold wrestled with a poncho for a few seconds before he pulled it over his head and returned to the covered dugout. French fans who stayed in their seats in the last few covered rows of the top of the stadium waved the country's flag, and many splashed around the concourse to pass time.

Others fled for the exits -- at the same stadium where last season's opener for the NFL's Philadelphia Eagles was delayed 65 minutes in the third quarter because of lightning -- as the delay dragged on, giving overseas spectators a triple dose of American sports nuisances: rain delays, overpriced concessions and TV timeouts in the form of hydration breaks. There was no second-half hydration break in this game after the long, wet pause in the action.

Weather aside, this game -- as so many do for France -- belonged to Mbappé as he tries to keep pace on the career goals list with new record holder Lionel Messi.

Playing in his third World Cup, the 27-year-old Mbappé matched Miroslav Klose of Germany on the goals list and moved one ahead of Brazil great Ronaldo.

Messi scored twice for Argentina earlier Monday to set the tournament record at 18. He had equaled Klose with his first World Cup hat trick in Argentina's 3-0 win over Algeria last Tuesday night in Kansas City, Missouri.

Mbappé scored his third goal of the tournament with a left-footed strike -- his supposed weaker foot -- from the edge of the box that got past Iraq goalkeeper Ahmed Basil, who got his first start in the tournament after captain Jalal Hassan allowed all four goals in Iraq's 4-1 loss to Norway.

For his second goal, Mbappé took advantage of a poor pass to the goalkeeper by Iraq defender Zaid Tahseen. Dembele controlled the loose ball and passed to Mbappé, who tapped it in with his right foot.

Mbappé had a chance at the hat trick on a late breakaway, but missed before he was subbed out at the 90-minute mark.

Mbappé helped France win the World Cup in 2018 and reach the final in 2022, when he was awarded the Silver Ball as the second-best player. Joined up front by Désiré Doué and Dembélé, France entered this year's tournament as a co-favorite with Spain.

France could return to Philadelphia to play Germany on July 4.

Iraq striker Aymen Hussein was subbed out with an apparent injury in the 26th minute of the first half and replaced by Ali Al-Hamadi. Hussein scored his 34th international goal in Iraq's World Cup opener.

Iraq is playing in the World Cup for just the second time after debuting in 1986.

stats.description: Total number of duels over the possession of the ball where a player loses the ball - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels for possession of the ball where possession was won (aerial won + ground duels won)

stats.name: groundDuelsWon

stats.name: duels

stats.name: groundDuels
stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.description: Total Expected Goals on Target excluding penalties

stats.name: bigChanceMissed
stats.description: A clear-cut scoring opportunity which was not converted/scored

stats.name: finalThirdEntries
stats.description: A pass where the ball moves from outside the final third into the final third

stats.name: penAreaEntries
stats.description: A pass where the ball moves from outside to inside the penalty area

stats.name: progressiveCarries
stats.description: Carries that significantly move the ball toward the opponent’s goal

stats.name: successfulFinalThirdPasses
stats.description: Total number of successful passes where the ball ends in the final third of the pitch (excludes throw-ins, keeper throws, and crosses)

stats.name: totalFinalThirdPasses
stats.description: Total number of passes (successful or unsuccessful) where ball ends in the final third of the pitch (excludes throw-ins, keeper throws, and crosses)

stats.name: touchesInOppBox
stats.description: Total touches inside the penalty area of the opposition

stats.name: blockedShots
stats.description: The number shots blocked by a defender.

stats.name: effectiveClearance
stats.description: The number of clearances that stops the attack of the opposing team.

stats.name: effectiveTackles
stats.description: The number of tackles where the player wins the ball.

stats.name: inneffectiveTackles
stats.description: The number of tackles where the player doesn't win the ball.

stats.name: interceptions
stats.description: The number of interceptions.

stats.name: tacklePct
stats.description: The perencentage of tackles where the player wins the ball.

stats.name: totalClearance
stats.description: The number of clearances attempted.

stats.name: totalTackles
stats.description: The number of tackles attempted.

stats.name: possWonAtt3rd
stats.description: Total number of times possession was regained in the attacking third of the field

stats.name: possWonMid3rd
stats.description: Total number of times possession was regained in the midfield third of the field

stats.name: ballRecovery
stats.description: When a player takes possession of a loose ball, or switch of possession between teams when the ball is in open play

stats.name: timesTackled
stats.description: The total number of times a player has been tackled.

stats.name: defensiveInterventions
stats.description: The sum of ball recoveries, won tackles, effective clearances, outfielder blocks, blocked crosses, and defensive third duels won.

stats.name: appearances
stats.description: The number of times a player has made an appearance in a match.

stats.name: avgRatingFromCorrespondent
stats.description: The rating from a correspondent for a player.

stats.name: avgRatingFromDataFeed
stats.description: The rating from the data feed for a player.

stats.description: The rating from the editor(s) for a player.

stats.name: avgRatingFromUser
stats.description: The average user rating for a player.

stats.name: dnp
stats.description: The number of times a player did not play in a match.

stats.name: draws
stats.description: The number of times the scored ended even for both teams in a match and there was no extra time.

stats.name: foulsCommitted
stats.description: The number of fouls comitted by a player or team.

stats.name: foulsSuffered
stats.description: The number of fouls suffered by a player or team.

stats.name: goalDifference
stats.description: The difference of goals scored and goals conceded.

stats.name: handBalls
stats.description: The number of handball fouls committed..

stats.name: losses
stats.description: The number of times a team has lost a match.

stats.name: lostCorners
stats.description: The number of corners conceded to the opposing team.

stats.name: minutes
stats.description: The number of minutes played by a player.

stats.name: ownGoals
stats.description: The number of own goals conceded.

stats.name: passPct
stats.description: The percentage of passes that result in the ball finding its target.

stats.name: redCards
stats.description: The number of red cards that have been issued.

stats.name: starts
stats.description: The number of starts a player has made in matches.

stats.name: subIns
stats.description: The number of times a player has come on a sub in a match.

stats.name: subOuts

stats.name: suspensions
stats.description: The number of matches a player has been suspended.

stats.description: The number of yellow cards accumulated.

stats.name: aerialsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels where the ball is in the air and possession is won

stats.name: duelsLost
stats.description: Total number of duels over the possession of the ball where a player loses the ball - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels for possession of the ball where possession was won (aerial won + ground duels won)

stats.name: groundDuelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels where possession is won and ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges.

stats.name: duels
stats.description: Total number of direct player-on-player confrontations for possession of the ball (aerial + ground duels)

stats.name: groundDuels
stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelWinPct
stats.description: The percentage of duels won.

stats.name: turnover
stats.description: An unsuccessful touch. A player only touched the ball and lost possession – bad control

stats.name: wonContest
stats.description: Total dribbles where a team player beats an opponent – no over-runs or defensive 1v1s

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)

stats.name: touches
stats.description: Total sum of a player's or team's on-the-ball events

stats.name: aerialDuelPct
stats.description: Percentage of aerial duels won against total contested duels.

stats.name: duelsLost
stats.description: Total number of duels over the possession of the ball where a player loses the ball - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels for possession of the ball where possession was won (aerial won + ground duels won)

stats.name: groundDuelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels where possession is won and ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges.

stats.name: duels
stats.description: Total number of direct player-on-player confrontations for possession of the ball (aerial + ground duels)

stats.name: groundDuels
stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelWinPct

stats.name: turnover

stats.name: wonContest
stats.description: Total dribbles where a team player beats an opponent – no over-runs or defensive 1v1s

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)
